title: The Wrong Blue
description: The sky in Maple Heights is the wrong blue. The Mage noticed it on day one. Casper saw it on the Coordinator's monitor. Not wrong like a mistake — wrong like someone picked the wrong hex code.

offer.speaker: Casper
offer.text: I remembered more. About the monitor. From when I got called in, before you got us out.

offer.speaker: Casper
offer.text: It was showing a place with a cave. Medieval. People fighting a wizard. And the sky in the video was the wrong blue. Not wrong like a mistake. Wrong like someone picked the wrong hex code.

offer.speaker: Casper
offer.text: I photographed it through the window. On my phone. You want to see?

accept.speaker: Casper
accept.text: The surplus shop girl has a color analysis app. She analyzes everything. She'll know what blue this is.

decline.speaker: Casper
decline.text: Okay. I'm keeping the photo though. Some things you keep even when nobody wants to look at them yet.

in_progress.speaker: Casper
in_progress.text: One digit off. She said our sky is one digit off from the sky in the video. Who picks a sky one digit off from another sky? On purpose?

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Madame Orrery
ready_to_turn_in.text: A photograph of a monitor showing a sky. Yes. Sit down. This is worth sitting for.

complete.speaker: Madame Orrery
complete.text: The monitor in the Coordinator's office is showing the current state of the previous world. In real time — or what passes for real time between worlds. Someone built that monitor to watch the last world while running this one.

complete.speaker: Madame Orrery
complete.text: And the sky was specified wrong. Not a mistake — a placeholder. A sky entered quickly, before someone finished thinking about what the world should look like.

complete.speaker: Madame Orrery
complete.text: The first thing anyone puts down when building a world is a sky. The sky shows where the world was when it was still being invented.

objectives.description: See Casper's photo of the Coordinator's monitor.
Have the surplus teenager run the color analysis.
Find the basement developer who can trace the monitor's signal.
Bring all of it to Madame Orrery.

cross_world_connection: W3: with hex_code_photo in inventory, the Lab Technician notices Cogsworth Junction's sky is #4B80D4 — two digits from W2. Orrery: 'Each world's sky is one digit removed from the last. If you extrapolate to World 6—' She stops. 'The sky in the last world won't have a color. It'll be pure white. Like a value that was never assigned.'
_wiring_notes: Gated entirely behind world2_relocated (Casper must be rescued; his giver spawn_flag doubles the gate). Dialogue content for the middle steps, wire into the talk interactions: surplus teen's analysis — 'Hex code #4A7FD4. That's the blue that shows up in stock photo libraries for default sky. The blue you get when you don't specify a sky. Ours is #4B80D5. One digit off. Someone picked ours very carefully.' basement_developer_w2 (new NPC, basement office, available once Casper's question-asking kid network exists): 'It's not a recording. It's a live feed. Same engine, different parameters.' — metadata timestamp sequence matches W1's day-night cycle. madame_orrery_w2 must exist for step 4: if the Orrery-chain W2 presence isn't placed yet, this quest is the forcing function — she sits in the ORRERY LIFE COACHING booth by the strip mall per the chain doc. NOTE: this quest only READS Orrery; the chain-step quest (fine_print) is deliberately NOT in this batch pending the fool_card_marks reconciliation question.
